title: Admin shell — chrome + auth gate for the /admin tree
problem: Every /admin/* surface needs the same wrapper: sidebar nav, language toggle, signed-in-as indicator, and the auth gate that bounces anon visitors to /login. Centralizing this in Admin.tsx as a layout component means each admin route is one focused screen, not a copy-pasted shell. Sub-routes render through <Outlet /> with the chrome already in place.
analysis: Layout component. useAuth() → if no email, <Navigate to='/login' replace />. Otherwise renders sidebar + header + <Outlet /> for sub-routes. useTenant() supplies tenant context for operator/buyer distinction; sub-routes decide what to show based on role. Mounted at /admin and /en/admin in router.tsx with index = AdminHub and children for /audit, /custodians, /inbox, /trash, /vouches, /showcase, /runbook. Marketplace-shaped surfaces (AdminFleet, AdminBilling, AdminTeam, AdminAppearance) are still reachable by direct URL per the root feature.json's 2026-05-09 note but no longer surfaced in the sidebar.

decisions.decision: Centralize the auth gate at the layout, not per-sub-route
decisions.rationale: Each /admin/* sub-route would otherwise need its own useAuth() guard. Centralizing means the gate is one component; missing it on a new sub-route is structurally impossible because the sub-route is under <Outlet />.
decisions.recommendation: When adding a new /admin sub-route, mount it as a child of Admin in router.tsx. Don't bypass the layout.

decisions.rationale: Per the root feature.json 2026-05-09 note: the marketplace surfaces (Fleet, Billing, Team, Appearance) cover a hypothetical buyer scenario. Removing them entirely would mean rewriting later if that scenario reopens. Hiding from sidebar removes the cost of carrying without losing the option.
decisions.recommendation: If the buyer scenario stays off the table for ~12 months, evaluate deleting these surfaces; until then, keep them parked.

successCriteria:
- Anon visitor on /admin/* is redirected to /login
- Signed-in operator sees the sidebar + sub-route content
- Sidebar reflects the active route
- Language toggle works in both /admin and /en/admin trees
- Sub-routes render via <Outlet /> with chrome already in place
implementation: src/pages/Admin.tsx — single layout component. useAuth + useTenant context reads. Sidebar items hardcoded; no dynamic role-based hiding today. <Outlet /> renders the active sub-route.
userGuide: Visit /admin (signed in) → land on AdminHub (the operator console index). Sub-routes: /admin/inbox (sessions queue), /admin/trash (soft-deleted), /admin/custodians (subscriptions), /admin/vouches (moderation), /admin/showcase (OG card grid), /admin/audit (action log), /admin/runbook (operator runbook).
knownLimitations: No role-based sidebar (every signed-in admin sees everything); fine for single-operator practice
Auth gate uses client-side redirect — there's a brief flash before /login renders. Acceptable
Marketplace-shaped surfaces (AdminFleet etc.) aren't lazy-removed; they ship in the bundle (~few KB)

statusHistory.reason: Layout shipped with the auth gate + sidebar.
Code-first doc co-located. Layout + auth gate stable; marketplace-shaped surface posture decision documented.

revisions.author: marc+claude
revisions.reason: Initial author — P3 admin sweep, originally deferred but documented per omega-done request.
